verses.text: Restore the fallen 'in the spirit of meekness'; 'bear ye one another's burdens.' Gentle love. We help, not crush, the stumbling brother.
Restore the fallen 'in the spirit of meekness'; 'bear ye one another's burdens.' Gentle love. We help, not crush, the stumbling brother.
Let every man prove his own work; 'every man shall bear his own burden.' Self-examination. Each answers for his own life before God.
Let every man prove his own work; 'every man shall bear his own burden.' Self-examination. Each answers for his own life before God.
Let every man prove his own work; 'every man shall bear his own burden.' Self-examination. Each answers for his own life before God.
Be not deceived; 'whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap'; sow to the Spirit and reap life. Sure harvest. We reap what we sow, for good or ill.
Be not deceived; 'whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap'; sow to the Spirit and reap life. Sure harvest. We reap what we sow, for good or ill.
Be not deceived; 'whatsoever a man soweth, that shall he also reap'; sow to the Spirit and reap life. Sure harvest. We reap what we sow, for good or ill.
'Let us not be weary in well doing'; do good to all, 'especially... the household of faith.' Persevere. Keep doing good, for the harvest comes.
'Let us not be weary in well doing'; do good to all, 'especially... the household of faith.' Persevere. Keep doing good, for the harvest comes.
They desire 'to make a fair shew in the flesh,' glorying in your circumcision. Empty show. The legalists sought their own boast.
They desire 'to make a fair shew in the flesh,' glorying in your circumcision. Empty show. The legalists sought their own boast.
They desire 'to make a fair shew in the flesh,' glorying in your circumcision. Empty show. The legalists sought their own boast.
'God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ'; 'a new creature' is all. The only boast. Christ's cross is the believer's sole glory.
'God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ'; 'a new creature' is all. The only boast. Christ's cross is the believer's sole glory.
'God forbid that I should glory, save in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ'; 'a new creature' is all. The only boast. Christ's cross is the believer's sole glory.
I bear 'the marks of the Lord Jesus'; the grace of Christ be with your spirit. Scars of service. His sufferings proved his apostleship.
